name: Maestri

runner.notes: macOS-only desktop app. Install agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, etc.) first, open the prepared harness worktree as a Maestri workspace working directory, create terminals for the agent(s), provide prompt.md, then run harness verification. Record guest agent/model separately from Maestri. Floors use APFS copy-on-write clones, not the same semantics as git worktree isolation in Orca/Tachyon.

inclusion: Direct Class A ADE competitor: native macOS orchestration canvas around BYO coding-agent CLIs with inter-agent PTY connections, workspace floors, and local-first privacy posture.
pricing_model: Free tier (1 workspace, unlimited agents); Pro lifetime license for unlimited workspaces (site shows free trial then one-time pay; regional pricing varies).
public_stack_signals: Official positioning: infinite spatial canvas for terminals, agents, notes, files, and sketches — not an AI agent itself.
Native macOS app (Swift/SwiftUI/AppKit, custom Metal canvas); no Electron/web views claimed; terminal stack associated with SwiftTerm.
Expects pre-installed coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, plain shell); agent-to-agent via Maestri Agent Skill over connections.
Floors: APFS copy-on-write isolated repo copies for branch/context switching; Remote SSH workspaces with reverse tunnel script.
Ombro on-device companion via Apple Foundation Models (requires newer macOS for full support); zero telemetry / no account claims.
capabilities_to_probe: inter-agent PTY connections and skill install
APFS floors isolation vs dirty worktree safety
workspace working-directory binding for harness worktrees
moat_hypotheses: spatial infinite-canvas UX for multi-agent operations
native Swift performance and macOS integration (Liquid Glass, Foundation Models)
agent-agnostic PTY connection model without cloud middleware
local-first privacy and lifetime pricing simplicity
setup_notes: Requires macOS 15.4+ and Apple Silicon per official download messaging.
Do not score from marketing screenshots alone; use the installed desktop app.
For fair comparison, point workspace working directory at the harness worktree only.
Guest agent CLIs must already be installed and authenticated.

sources.notes: English product landing: orchestration canvas positioning, agent connections, floors, Ombro, portals, routines, Swift/native stack, privacy, free/Pro pricing, macOS download.
Portuguese landing (default for many locales); same product claims as EN surface.
Defines Maestri as canvas/orchestration layer around existing agents; core concepts workspace/canvas/terminal/connection/note/file tree; macOS only.
Terminals host coding agents; role instructions per terminal; expects agents pre-installed.
Inter-agent communication via Maestri Agent Skill; agent-agnostic CLI-level prompting across harnesses.
APFS copy-on-write floors as isolated branch/workspace copies with own terminals.
Workspace as project container with working directory, layout, multi-workspace switching.
Scheduled prompts to agent terminals at intervals.
Embedded WebKit portals; agents can control connected portals.
On-device companion monitoring agents, summaries, next-step suggestions via Apple Foundation Models.
Remote SSH workspaces with reverse tunnel and inspectable remote script; no claimed always-on daemon install.

research.positioning: Native macOS infinite-canvas orchestration environment where BYO coding-agent terminals are spatial nodes that can connect, share notes, and run isolated floors — the conductor layer, not the agent itself.

runtime: native terminal emulation (SwiftTerm lineage per public product claims)
frontend: Liquid Glass / native macOS UI conventions
backend: optional SSH reverse tunnel for remote workspaces
unknowns: Closed-source internals beyond official docs; no public source_url mapped.
Exact agent preset list and skill injection mechanism beyond docs summary.
Windows/Linux support not offered; Linux harness hosts cannot run the app natively.

infrastructure: Local macOS app owns canvas, workspaces, terminals, and local notes.
Coding agents run as local CLI processes inside terminals; provider traffic follows each agent’s own privacy policy.
Floors create APFS copy-on-write isolated working copies for branch/context switches.
Optional SSH mode installs a small remote script and reverse tunnel for cross-machine agent communication.
Ombro companion uses on-device Apple Foundation Models when available.

agent_support: BYO CLI agents via terminal presets
Maestri Agent Skill for connected agents
workspace_isolation: APFS floors as isolated repo copies
review_shipping: open project in VS Code/Zed/Xcode from workspace
agent-written sticky notes for progress/review text
human spatial review of multiple agent terminals
remote_mobile: Ombro floating companion window on the same Mac
no dedicated phone companion mapped in official docs reviewed
integrations: VS Code / Zed / Xcode open shortcuts

install_surface: Maestri DMG for macOS Apple Silicon
guest agent CLI installs (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, etc.)
workspace pointing at prepared harness worktree
parity_risks: macOS-only product; cannot be run on typical Linux CI hosts without a Mac runner.
APFS floors isolation is not the same as git worktree race/merge semantics used by Orca/Fusion/Tachyon probes.
Spatial orchestration and agent-to-agent PTY may not exercise plan/verify/merge gates the suite optimizes for.
Closed source: stack claims limited to official site/docs; no source_url.
Guest agent quality dominates single-task correctness; measure Maestri as orchestration environment, not as the coding model.

hypotheses: Canvas-native multi-agent UX can reduce operator overload versus tabbed terminals.
Native Swift stack and on-device Ombro may differentiate from Electron ADE cockpits on macOS.
Agent-agnostic PTY connections lower glue-code cost between heterogeneous CLIs.
Lifetime pricing and no-account local posture can attract individual power users.
evidence: Official docs state Maestri is not an agent but an orchestration canvas around Claude Code/Codex/OpenCode.
Official docs document connections with Maestri Agent Skill, floors via APFS, routines, portals, SSH, and Ombro.
Official site claims Swift/SwiftUI, Metal canvas, zero telemetry, no account, free and Pro tiers.
unknowns: Hands-on isolation quality under dirty worktrees and multi-agent collision needs a Mac-hosted bench run.
How much Maestri improves verification/evidence capture versus raw CLIs remains unmeasured.
